title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Delivery Incident Response
summary: Delivery slows while storage cost grows, teams rerun failures without diagnosis, logs expose identifiers, exceptions never expire, and no one can reconstruct which change reached which users. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci delivery incident response increment in delivery operations and governance defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.
Reconstruct event, workflow version, runner, inputs, dependency graph, first causal failure, retries, side effects, and downstream state before rerunning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:

  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Delivery Incident Response · Theory Ci Delivery Incident Response
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Delivery Incident Response contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Ci Delivery Incident Response · Theory Ci Delivery Incident Response.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls. For Predict Ci Delivery Incident Response · Theory Ci Delivery Incident Response, keep Ci Delivery Incident Response connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

At Predict Ci Delivery Incident Response · Theory Ci Delivery Incident Response, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A theory delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk.

For Predict Ci Delivery Incident Response · Theory Ci Delivery Incident Response, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Cancelling the active workflow fully contains a delivery incident.

options.text: Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Cancelling the active workflow fully contains a delivery incident. That claim fails the predict ci delivery incident response evidence boundary for Ci Delivery Incident Response.

For predict ci delivery incident response, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof. This does not satisfy Ci Delivery Incident Response for delivery operations and governance defense.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Ci Delivery Incident Response while producing delivery operations and governance defense.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls. A defensible solution applies Ci Delivery Incident Response, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery operations and governance defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-delivery-incident-response — Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-4
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-4
    artifact: delivery-operations.yaml
    decision: worked ci observability incidents governance 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      metrics: queue-critical-path-failure-class-recovery
      cardinality: bounded
      privacy: actor-and-input-minimized
      retention: risk-cost-policy
      incident: contain-preserve-restore-notify
      first-causal-failure: reconstructed
      credential-response: revoke-and-rotate
      artifact-recovery: prior-verified-digest
      exception: owner-reason-expiry
      drill: quarterly
      improvement: outcome-reviewed
    verify: reconstruct one delivery incident from event workflow runner dependency artifact deployment telemetry audit containment and recovery evidence
    failure: Reject unbounded metric labels, forever retention, blind reruns, workflow cancellation as containment, or non-expiring central policy exceptions.
    repair: Measure useful flow safely, retain by need, diagnose first cause, contain authority and mutations, restore verified artifacts, expire exceptions, and drill.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Cancelling the active workflow fully contains a delivery incident. This fails the Ci Delivery Incident Response contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
